              - paragraph [ref=e1958]:
                - text: Some TCP implementations have included a FLUSH call, which will empty the TCP send queue of any data that the user has issued SEND calls for but is still to the right of the current send window. That is, it flushes as much queued send data as possible without losing sequence number synchronization. The FLUSH call MAY be implemented (MAY-14).
                - link "¶" [ref=e1959] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.7-1"
            - generic [ref=e1961]:
              - heading "3.9.1.8. Asynchronous Reports" [level=5] [ref=e1962]:
                - link "3.9.1.8." [ref=e1963] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8"
                - link "Asynchronous Reports" [ref=e1964] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-asynchronous-reports"
              - paragraph [ref=e1965]:
                - text: "There MUST be a mechanism for reporting soft TCP error conditions to the application (MUST-47). Generically, we assume this takes the form of an application-supplied ERROR_REPORT routine that may be upcalled asynchronously from the transport layer:"
                - link "¶" [ref=e1966] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8-1"
              - list [ref=e1967]:
                - listitem [ref=e1968]:
                  - text: ERROR_REPORT(local connection name, reason, subreason)
                  - link "¶" [ref=e1969] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8-2.1"
              - paragraph [ref=e1970]:
                - text: "The precise encoding of the reason and subreason parameters is not specified here. However, the conditions that are reported asynchronously to the application MUST include:"
                - link "¶" [ref=e1971] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8-3"
              - list [ref=e1972]:
                - listitem [ref=e1973]:
                  - text: ICMP error message arrived (see
                  - link "Section 3.9.2.2" [ref=e1974] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#icmp"
                  - text: for description of handling each ICMP message type since some message types need to be suppressed from generating reports to the application)
                  - link "¶" [ref=e1975] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8-4.1"
                - listitem [ref=e1976]:
                  - text: Excessive retransmissions (see
                  - link "Section 3.8.3" [ref=e1977] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#connfail"
                  - text: )
                  - link "¶" [ref=e1978] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8-4.2"
                - listitem [ref=e1979]:
                  - text: Urgent pointer advance (see
                  - link "Section 3.8.5" [ref=e1980] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#urgent"
                  - text: )
                  - link "¶" [ref=e1981] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8-4.3"
              - paragraph [ref=e1982]:
                - text: However, an application program that does not want to receive such ERROR_REPORT calls SHOULD be able to effectively disable these calls (SHLD-20).
                - link "¶" [ref=e1983] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.8-5"
            - generic [ref=e1984]:
              - heading "{{REDACTED_IP}}. Set Differentiated Services Field (IPv4 TOS or IPv6 Traffic Class)" [level=5] [ref=e1985]:
                - link "3.9.1.9." [ref=e1986] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.9"
                - link "Set Differentiated Services Field (IPv4 TOS or IPv6 Traffic Class)" [ref=e1987] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-set-differentiated-services"
              - paragraph [ref=e1988]:
                - text: The application layer MUST be able to specify the Differentiated Services field for segments that are sent on a connection (MUST-48). The Differentiated Services field includes the 6-bit Differentiated Services Codepoint (DSCP) value. It is not required, but the application SHOULD be able to change the Differentiated Services field during the connection lifetime (SHLD-21). TCP implementations SHOULD pass the current Differentiated Services field value without change to the IP layer, when it sends segments on the connection (SHLD-22).
                - link "¶" [ref=e1989] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.9-1"
              - paragraph [ref=e1990]:
                - text: The Differentiated Services field will be specified independently in each direction on the connection, so that the receiver application will specify the Differentiated Services field used for ACK segments.
                - link "¶" [ref=e1991] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.9-2"
              - paragraph [ref=e1992]:
                - text: TCP implementations MAY pass the most recently received Differentiated Services field up to the application (MAY-9).
                - link "¶" [ref=e1993] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.1.9-3"
          - generic [ref=e1994]:
            - heading "3.9.2. TCP/Lower-Level Interface" [level=4] [ref=e1995]:
              - link "3.9.2." [ref=e1996] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.9.2"
              - link "TCP/Lower-Level Interface" [ref=e1997] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-tcp-lower-level-interface"
            - paragraph [ref=e1998]:
              - text: The TCP endpoint calls on a lower-level protocol module to actually send and receive information over a network. The two current standard Internet Protocol (IP) versions layered below TCP are IPv4
              - generic [ref=e1999]:
                - text: "["
                - link "1" [ref=e2000] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc791
                - text: "]"
              - text: and IPv6
              - generic [ref=e2001]:
                - text: "["
                - link "13" [ref=e2002] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8200
                - text: "]"
              - text: .
              - link "¶" [ref=e2003] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.9.2-1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2004]:
              - text: "If the lower-level protocol is IPv4, it provides arguments for a type of service (used within the Differentiated Services field) and for a time to live. TCP uses the following settings for these parameters:"
              - link "¶" [ref=e2005] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.9.2-2"
            - generic [ref=e2006]:
              - term [ref=e2007]: "Diffserv field:"
              - definition [ref=e2008]:
                - text: The IP header value for the Diffserv field is given by the user. This includes the bits of the Diffserv Codepoint (DSCP).
                - link "¶" [ref=e2009] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2-3.2"
              - term [ref=e2010]: "Time to Live (TTL):"
              - definition [ref=e2011]:
                - paragraph [ref=e2012]:
                  - text: The TTL value used to send TCP segments MUST be configurable (MUST-49).
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2013] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.2-3.4.1"
                - list [ref=e2014]:
                  - listitem [ref=e2015]:
                    - text: Note that RFC 793 specified one minute (60 seconds) as a constant for the TTL because the assumed maximum segment lifetime was two minutes. This was intended to explicitly ask that a segment be destroyed if it could not be delivered by the internet system within one minute. RFC 1122 updated RFC 793 to require that the TTL be configurable.
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2016] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.9.2-3.4.2.1"
                  - listitem [ref=e2017]:
                    - text: Note that the Diffserv field is permitted to change during a connection (Section
                    - link "{{REDACTED_IP}}" [ref=e2018] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1122#section-4.2.4.2
                    - text: of RFC 1122). However, the application interface might not support this ability, and the application does not have knowledge about individual TCP segments, so this can only be done on a coarse granularity, at best. This limitation is further discussed in RFC 7657 (Sections
                    - link "5.1" [ref=e2019] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7657#section-5.1
                    - text: ","
                    - link "5.3" [ref=e2020] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7657#section-5.3
                    - text: ", and"
                    - link "6" [ref=e2021] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7657#section-6
                    - text: )
                    - generic [ref=e2022]:
                      - text: "["
                      - link "50" [ref=e2023] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7657
                      - text: "]"
                    - text: . Generally, an application SHOULD NOT change the Diffserv field value during the course of a connection (SHLD-23).
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2024] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.9.2-3.4.2.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2025]:
              - text: Any lower-level protocol will have to provide the source address, destination address, and protocol fields, and some way to determine the "TCP length", both to provide the functional equivalent service of IP and to be used in the TCP checksum.
              - link "¶" [ref=e2026] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.9.2-4"
            - paragraph [ref=e2027]:
              - text: When received options are passed up to TCP from the IP layer, a TCP implementation MUST ignore options that it does not understand (MUST-50).
              - link "¶" [ref=e2028] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.9.2-5"
            - paragraph [ref=e2029]:
              - text: A TCP implementation MAY support the Timestamp (MAY-10) and Record Route (MAY-11) Options.
              - link "¶" [ref=e2030] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.9.2-6"
            - generic [ref=e2031]:
              - heading "3.9.2.1. Source Routing" [level=5] [ref=e2032]:
                - link "3.9.2.1." [ref=e2033] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.1"
                - link "Source Routing" [ref=e2034] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-source-routing"
              - paragraph [ref=e2035]:
                - text: If the lower level is IP (or other protocol that provides this feature) and source routing is used, the interface must allow the route information to be communicated. This is especially important so that the source and destination addresses used in the TCP checksum be the originating source and ultimate destination. It is also important to preserve the return route to answer connection requests.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2036] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.1-1"
              - paragraph [ref=e2037]:
                - text: An application MUST be able to specify a source route when it actively opens a TCP connection (MUST-51), and this MUST take precedence over a source route received in a datagram (MUST-52).
                - link "¶" [ref=e2038] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.1-2"
              - paragraph [ref=e2039]:
                - text: When a TCP connection is OPENed passively and a packet arrives with a completed IP Source Route Option (containing a return route), TCP implementations MUST save the return route and use it for all segments sent on this connection (MUST-53). If a different source route arrives in a later segment, the later definition SHOULD override the earlier one (SHLD-24).
                - link "¶" [ref=e2040] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.1-3"
            - generic [ref=e2042]:
              - heading "3.9.2.2. ICMP Messages" [level=5] [ref=e2043]:
                - link "3.9.2.2." [ref=e2044] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2"
                - link "ICMP Messages" [ref=e2045] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-icmp-messages"
              - paragraph [ref=e2046]:
                - text: TCP implementations MUST act on an ICMP error message passed up from the IP layer, directing it to the connection that created the error (MUST-54). The necessary demultiplexing information can be found in the IP header contained within the ICMP message.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2047] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-1"
              - paragraph [ref=e2048]:
                - text: This applies to ICMPv6 in addition to IPv4 ICMP.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2049] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-2"
              - paragraph [ref=e2050]:
                - generic [ref=e2051]:
                  - text: "["
                  - link "35" [ref=e2052] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5461
                  - text: "]"
                - text: "contains discussion of specific ICMP and ICMPv6 messages classified as either \"soft\" or \"hard\" errors that may bear different responses. Treatment for classes of ICMP messages is described below:"
                - link "¶" [ref=e2053] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-3"
              - generic [ref=e2054]:
                - term [ref=e2055]: Source Quench
                - definition [ref=e2056]:
                  - text: TCP implementations MUST silently discard any received ICMP Source Quench messages (MUST-55). See
                  - generic [ref=e2057]:
                    - text: "["
                    - link "11" [ref=e2058] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6633
                    - text: "]"
                  - text: for discussion.
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2059] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-4.2"
                - term [ref=e2060]: Soft Errors
                - definition [ref=e2061]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2062]:
                    - text: "For IPv4 ICMP, these include: Destination Unreachable -- codes 0, 1, 5; Time Exceeded -- codes 0, 1; and Parameter Problem."
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2063] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-4.4.1"
                  - paragraph [ref=e2064]:
                    - text: "For ICMPv6, these include: Destination Unreachable -- codes 0, 3; Time Exceeded -- codes 0, 1; and Parameter Problem -- codes 0, 1, 2."
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2065] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-4.4.2"
                  - paragraph [ref=e2066]:
                    - text: Since these Unreachable messages indicate soft error conditions, a TCP implementation MUST NOT abort the connection (MUST-56), and it SHOULD make the information available to the application (SHLD-25).
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2067] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-4.4.3"
                - term [ref=e2068]: Hard Errors
                - definition [ref=e2069]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2070]:
                    - text: For ICMP these include Destination Unreachable -- codes 2-4.
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2071] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-4.6.1"
                  - paragraph [ref=e2072]:
                    - text: These are hard error conditions, so TCP implementations SHOULD abort the connection (SHLD-26).
                    - generic [ref=e2073]:
                      - text: "["
                      - link "35" [ref=e2074] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5461
                      - text: "]"
                    - text: notes that some implementations do not abort connections when an ICMP hard error is received for a connection that is in any of the synchronized states.
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2075] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-4.6.2"
              - paragraph [ref=e2076]:
                - text: Note that
                - generic [ref=e2077]:
                  - text: "["
                  - link "35" [ref=e2078] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5461
                  - text: "],"
                  - link "Section 4" [ref=e2079] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5461#section-4
                - text: describes widespread implementation behavior that treats soft errors as hard errors during connection establishment.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2080] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.2-5"
            - generic [ref=e2081]:
              - heading "{{REDACTED_IP}}. Source Address Validation" [level=5] [ref=e2082]:
                - link "3.9.2.3." [ref=e2083] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-{{REDACTED_IP}}"
                - link "Source Address Validation" [ref=e2084] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-source-address-validation"
              - paragraph [ref=e2085]:
                - text: "RFC 1122 requires addresses to be validated in incoming SYN packets:"
                - link "¶" [ref=e2086] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.3-1"
              - blockquote [ref=e2087]:
                - paragraph [ref=e2088]:
                  - text: An incoming SYN with an invalid source address MUST be ignored either by TCP or by the IP layer [(MUST-63)] (see Section
                  - link "3.2.1.3" [ref=e2089] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1122#section-3.2.1.3
                  - text: ).
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2090] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.2.3-2.1"
                - paragraph [ref=e2091]:
                  - text: A TCP implementation MUST silently discard an incoming SYN segment that is addressed to a broadcast or multicast address [(MUST-57)].
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2092] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.9.2.3-2.2"
              - paragraph [ref=e2093]:
                - text: This prevents connection state and replies from being erroneously generated, and implementers should note that this guidance is applicable to all incoming segments, not just SYNs, as specifically indicated in RFC 1122.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2094] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.9.2.3-3"
        - generic [ref=e2095]:
          - heading "3.10. Event Processing" [level=3] [ref=e2096]:
            - link "3.10." [ref=e2097] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10"
            - link "Event Processing" [ref=e2098] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#name-event-processing"
          - paragraph [ref=e2099]:
            - text: The processing depicted in this section is an example of one possible implementation. Other implementations may have slightly different processing sequences, but they should differ from those in this section only in detail, not in substance.
            - link "¶" [ref=e2100] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-1"
          - paragraph [ref=e2101]:
            - text: "The activity of the TCP endpoint can be characterized as responding to events. The events that occur can be cast into three categories: user calls, arriving segments, and timeouts. This section describes the processing the TCP endpoint does in response to each of the events. In many cases, the processing required depends on the state of the connection."
            - link "¶" [ref=e2102] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-2"
          - paragraph [ref=e2103]:
            - text: "Events that occur:"
            - link "¶" [ref=e2104] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-3"
          - list [ref=e2105]:
            - listitem [ref=e2106]:
              - paragraph [ref=e2107]:
                - text: User Calls
                - link "¶" [ref=e2108] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10-4.1.1"
              - list [ref=e2109]:
                - listitem [ref=e2110]:
                  - text: OPEN
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2111] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.1.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2112]:
                  - text: SEND
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2113] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.1.2.2"
                - listitem [ref=e2114]:
                  - text: RECEIVE
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2115] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.1.2.3"
                - listitem [ref=e2116]:
                  - text: CLOSE
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2117] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.1.2.4"
                - listitem [ref=e2118]:
                  - text: ABORT
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2119] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.1.2.5"
                - listitem [ref=e2120]:
                  - text: STATUS
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2121] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.1.2.6"
            - listitem [ref=e2122]:
              - paragraph [ref=e2123]:
                - text: Arriving Segments
                - link "¶" [ref=e2124] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10-4.2.1"
              - list [ref=e2125]:
                - listitem [ref=e2126]:
                  - text: SEGMENT ARRIVES
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2127] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.2.2.1"
            - listitem [ref=e2128]:
              - paragraph [ref=e2129]:
                - text: Timeouts
                - link "¶" [ref=e2130] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10-4.3.1"
              - list [ref=e2131]:
                - listitem [ref=e2132]:
                  - text: USER TIMEOUT
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2133] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.3.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2134]:
                  - text: RETRANSMISSION TIMEOUT
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2135] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.3.2.2"
                - listitem [ref=e2136]:
                  - text: TIME-WAIT TIMEOUT
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2137] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10-4.3.2.3"
          - paragraph [ref=e2138]:
            - text: The model of the TCP/user interface is that user commands receive an immediate return and possibly a delayed response via an event or pseudo-interrupt. In the following descriptions, the term "signal" means cause a delayed response.
            - link "¶" [ref=e2139] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-5"
          - paragraph [ref=e2140]:
            - text: "Error responses in this document are identified by character strings. For example, user commands referencing connections that do not exist receive \"error: connection not open\"."
            - link "¶" [ref=e2141] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-6"
          - paragraph [ref=e2142]:
            - text: Please note in the following that all arithmetic on sequence numbers, acknowledgment numbers, windows, et cetera, is modulo 2
            - superscript [ref=e2143]: "32"
            - text: (the size of the sequence number space). Also note that "=<" means less than or equal to (modulo 2
            - superscript [ref=e2144]: "32"
            - text: ).
            - link "¶" [ref=e2145] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-7"
          - paragraph [ref=e2146]:
            - text: A natural way to think about processing incoming segments is to imagine that they are first tested for proper sequence number (i.e., that their contents lie in the range of the expected "receive window" in the sequence number space) and then that they are generally queued and processed in sequence number order.
            - link "¶" [ref=e2147] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-8"
          - paragraph [ref=e2148]:
            - text: When a segment overlaps other already received segments, we reconstruct the segment to contain just the new data and adjust the header fields to be consistent.
            - link "¶" [ref=e2149] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-9"
          - paragraph [ref=e2150]:
            - text: Note that if no state change is mentioned, the TCP connection stays in the same state.
            - link "¶" [ref=e2151] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-3.10-10"
          - generic [ref=e2152]:
            - heading "3.10.1. OPEN Call" [level=4] [ref=e2153]:
              - link "3.10.1." [ref=e2154] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1"
              - link "OPEN Call" [ref=e2155] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-open-call"
            - paragraph [ref=e2156]:
              - text: CLOSED STATE (i.e., TCB does not exist)
              - link "¶" [ref=e2157] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-1"
            - list [ref=e2158]:
              - listitem [ref=e2159]:
                - text: "Create a new transmission control block (TCB) to hold connection state information. Fill in local socket identifier, remote socket, Diffserv field, security/compartment, and user timeout information. Note that some parts of the remote socket may be unspecified in a passive OPEN and are to be filled in by the parameters of the incoming SYN segment. Verify the security and Diffserv value requested are allowed for this user, if not, return \"error: Diffserv value not allowed\" or \"error: security/compartment not allowed\". If passive, enter the LISTEN state and return. If active and the remote socket is unspecified, return \"error: remote socket unspecified\"; if active and the remote socket is specified, issue a SYN segment. An initial send sequence number (ISS) is selected. A SYN segment of the form <SEQ=ISS><CTL=SYN> is sent. Set SND.UNA to ISS, SND.NXT to ISS+1, enter SYN-SENT state, and return."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2160] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.1-2.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2161]:
                - text: "If the caller does not have access to the local socket specified, return \"error: connection illegal for this process\". If there is no room to create a new connection, return \"error: insufficient resources\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2162] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.1-2.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2163]:
              - text: LISTEN STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2164] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-3"
            - list [ref=e2165]:
              - listitem [ref=e2166]:
                - text: "If the OPEN call is active and the remote socket is specified, then change the connection from passive to active, select an ISS. Send a SYN segment, set SND.UNA to ISS, SND.NXT to ISS+1. Enter SYN-SENT state. Data associated with SEND may be sent with SYN segment or queued for transmission after entering ESTABLISHED state. The urgent bit if requested in the command must be sent with the data segments sent as a result of this command. If there is no room to queue the request, respond with \"error: insufficient resources\". If the remote socket was not specified, then return \"error: remote socket unspecified\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2167] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.1-4.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2168]:
              - text: SYN-SENT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2169] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-5"
            - paragraph [ref=e2170]:
              - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2171] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-6"
            - paragraph [ref=e2172]:
              - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2173] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-7"
            - paragraph [ref=e2174]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2175] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-8"
            - paragraph [ref=e2176]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2177] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-9"
            - paragraph [ref=e2178]:
              - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2179] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-10"
            - paragraph [ref=e2180]:
              - text: CLOSING STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2181] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-11"
            - paragraph [ref=e2182]:
              - text: LAST-ACK STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2183] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-12"
            - paragraph [ref=e2184]:
              - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2185] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.1-13"
            - list [ref=e2186]:
              - listitem [ref=e2187]:
                - text: "Return \"error: connection already exists\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2188] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.1-14.1"
          - generic [ref=e2189]:
            - heading "3.10.2. SEND Call" [level=4] [ref=e2190]:
              - link "3.10.2." [ref=e2191] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2"
              - link "SEND Call" [ref=e2192] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-send-call"
            - paragraph [ref=e2193]:
              - text: CLOSED STATE (i.e., TCB does not exist)
              - link "¶" [ref=e2194] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-1"
            - list [ref=e2195]:
              - listitem [ref=e2196]:
                - text: "If the user does not have access to such a connection, then return \"error: connection illegal for this process\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2197] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.2-2.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2198]:
                - text: "Otherwise, return \"error: connection does not exist\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2199] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.2-2.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2200]:
              - text: LISTEN STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2201] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-3"
            - list [ref=e2202]:
              - listitem [ref=e2203]:
                - text: "If the remote socket is specified, then change the connection from passive to active, select an ISS. Send a SYN segment, set SND.UNA to ISS, SND.NXT to ISS+1. Enter SYN-SENT state. Data associated with SEND may be sent with SYN segment or queued for transmission after entering ESTABLISHED state. The urgent bit if requested in the command must be sent with the data segments sent as a result of this command. If there is no room to queue the request, respond with \"error: insufficient resources\". If the remote socket was not specified, then return \"error: remote socket unspecified\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2204] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.2-4.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2205]:
              - text: SYN-SENT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2206] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-5"
            - paragraph [ref=e2207]:
              - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2208] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-6"
            - list [ref=e2209]:
              - listitem [ref=e2210]:
                - text: "Queue the data for transmission after entering ESTABLISHED state. If no space to queue, respond with \"error: insufficient resources\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2211] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.2-7.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2212]:
              - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2213] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-8"
            - paragraph [ref=e2214]:
              - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2215] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-9"
            - list [ref=e2216]:
              - listitem [ref=e2217]:
                - text: "Segmentize the buffer and send it with a piggybacked acknowledgment (acknowledgment value = RCV.NXT). If there is insufficient space to remember this buffer, simply return \"error: insufficient resources\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2218] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.2-10.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2219]:
                - text: If the URGENT flag is set, then SND.UP <- SND.NXT and set the urgent pointer in the outgoing segments.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2220] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.2-10.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2221]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2222] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-11"
            - paragraph [ref=e2223]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2224] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-12"
            - paragraph [ref=e2225]:
              - text: CLOSING STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2226] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-13"
            - paragraph [ref=e2227]:
              - text: LAST-ACK STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2228] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-14"
            - paragraph [ref=e2229]:
              - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2230] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.2-15"
            - list [ref=e2231]:
              - listitem [ref=e2232]:
                - text: "Return \"error: connection closing\" and do not service request."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2233] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.2-16.1"
          - generic [ref=e2234]:
            - heading "3.10.3. RECEIVE Call" [level=4] [ref=e2235]:
              - link "3.10.3." [ref=e2236] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3"
              - link "RECEIVE Call" [ref=e2237] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-receive-call"
            - paragraph [ref=e2238]:
              - text: CLOSED STATE (i.e., TCB does not exist)
              - link "¶" [ref=e2239] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-1"
            - list [ref=e2240]:
              - listitem [ref=e2241]:
                - text: "If the user does not have access to such a connection, return \"error: connection illegal for this process\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2242] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-2.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2243]:
                - text: "Otherwise, return \"error: connection does not exist\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2244] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-2.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2245]:
              - text: LISTEN STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2246] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-3"
            - paragraph [ref=e2247]:
              - text: SYN-SENT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2248] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-4"
            - paragraph [ref=e2249]:
              - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2250] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-5"
            - list [ref=e2251]:
              - listitem [ref=e2252]:
                - text: "Queue for processing after entering ESTABLISHED state. If there is no room to queue this request, respond with \"error: insufficient resources\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2253] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-6.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2254]:
              - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2255] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-7"
            - paragraph [ref=e2256]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2257] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-8"
            - paragraph [ref=e2258]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2259] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-9"
            - list [ref=e2260]:
              - listitem [ref=e2261]:
                - text: "If insufficient incoming segments are queued to satisfy the request, queue the request. If there is no queue space to remember the RECEIVE, respond with \"error: insufficient resources\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2262] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-10.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2263]:
                - text: Reassemble queued incoming segments into receive buffer and return to user. Mark "push seen" (PUSH) if this is the case.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2264] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-10.2"
              - listitem [ref=e2265]:
                - text: If RCV.UP is in advance of the data currently being passed to the user, notify the user of the presence of urgent data.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2266] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-10.3"
              - listitem [ref=e2267]:
                - text: When the TCP endpoint takes responsibility for delivering data to the user, that fact must be communicated to the sender via an acknowledgment. The formation of such an acknowledgment is described below in the discussion of processing an incoming segment.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2268] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-10.4"
            - paragraph [ref=e2269]:
              - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2270] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-11"
            - list [ref=e2271]:
              - listitem [ref=e2272]:
                - text: "Since the remote side has already sent FIN, RECEIVEs must be satisfied by data already on hand, but not yet delivered to the user. If no text is awaiting delivery, the RECEIVE will get an \"error: connection closing\" response. Otherwise, any remaining data can be used to satisfy the RECEIVE."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2273] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-12.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2274]:
              - text: CLOSING STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2275] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-13"
            - paragraph [ref=e2276]:
              - text: LAST-ACK STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2277] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-14"
            - paragraph [ref=e2278]:
              - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2279] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.3-15"
            - list [ref=e2280]:
              - listitem [ref=e2281]:
                - text: "Return \"error: connection closing\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2282] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.3-16.1"
          - generic [ref=e2283]:
            - heading "3.10.4. CLOSE Call" [level=4] [ref=e2284]:
              - link "3.10.4." [ref=e2285] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4"
              - link "CLOSE Call" [ref=e2286] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-close-call"
            - paragraph [ref=e2287]:
              - text: CLOSED STATE (i.e., TCB does not exist)
              - link "¶" [ref=e2288] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-1"
            - list [ref=e2289]:
              - listitem [ref=e2290]:
                - text: "If the user does not have access to such a connection, return \"error: connection illegal for this process\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2291] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-2.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2292]:
                - text: "Otherwise, return \"error: connection does not exist\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2293] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-2.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2294]:
              - text: LISTEN STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2295] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-3"
            - list [ref=e2296]:
              - listitem [ref=e2297]:
                - text: "Any outstanding RECEIVEs are returned with \"error: closing\" responses. Delete TCB, enter CLOSED state, and return."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2298] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-4.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2299]:
              - text: SYN-SENT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2300] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-5"
            - list [ref=e2301]:
              - listitem [ref=e2302]:
                - text: "Delete the TCB and return \"error: closing\" responses to any queued SENDs, or RECEIVEs."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2303] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-6.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2304]:
              - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2305] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-7"
            - list [ref=e2306]:
              - listitem [ref=e2307]:
                - text: If no SENDs have been issued and there is no pending data to send, then form a FIN segment and send it, and enter FIN-WAIT-1 state; otherwise, queue for processing after entering ESTABLISHED state.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2308] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-8.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2309]:
              - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2310] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-9"
            - list [ref=e2311]:
              - listitem [ref=e2312]:
                - text: Queue this until all preceding SENDs have been segmentized, then form a FIN segment and send it. In any case, enter FIN-WAIT-1 state.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2313] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-10.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2314]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2315] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-11"
            - paragraph [ref=e2316]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2317] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-12"
            - list [ref=e2318]:
              - listitem [ref=e2319]:
                - text: "Strictly speaking, this is an error and should receive an \"error: connection closing\" response. An \"ok\" response would be acceptable, too, as long as a second FIN is not emitted (the first FIN may be retransmitted, though)."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2320] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-13.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2321]:
              - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2322] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-14"
            - list [ref=e2323]:
              - listitem [ref=e2324]:
                - text: Queue this request until all preceding SENDs have been segmentized; then send a FIN segment, enter LAST-ACK state.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2325] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-15.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2326]:
              - text: CLOSING STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2327] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-16"
            - paragraph [ref=e2328]:
              - text: LAST-ACK STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2329] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-17"
            - paragraph [ref=e2330]:
              - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2331] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.4-18"
            - list [ref=e2332]:
              - listitem [ref=e2333]:
                - text: "Respond with \"error: connection closing\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2334] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.4-19.1"
          - generic [ref=e2335]:
            - heading "3.10.5. ABORT Call" [level=4] [ref=e2336]:
              - link "3.10.5." [ref=e2337] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5"
              - link "ABORT Call" [ref=e2338] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-abort-call"
            - paragraph [ref=e2339]:
              - text: CLOSED STATE (i.e., TCB does not exist)
              - link "¶" [ref=e2340] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-1"
            - list [ref=e2341]:
              - listitem [ref=e2342]:
                - text: "If the user should not have access to such a connection, return \"error: connection illegal for this process\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2343] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.5-2.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2344]:
                - text: "Otherwise, return \"error: connection does not exist\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2345] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.5-2.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2346]:
              - text: LISTEN STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2347] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-3"
            - list [ref=e2348]:
              - listitem [ref=e2349]:
                - text: "Any outstanding RECEIVEs should be returned with \"error: connection reset\" responses. Delete TCB, enter CLOSED state, and return."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2350] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.5-4.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2351]:
              - text: SYN-SENT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2352] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-5"
            - list [ref=e2353]:
              - listitem [ref=e2354]:
                - text: All queued SENDs and RECEIVEs should be given "connection reset" notification. Delete the TCB, enter CLOSED state, and return.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2355] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.5-6.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2356]:
              - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2357] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-7"
            - paragraph [ref=e2358]:
              - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2359] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-8"
            - paragraph [ref=e2360]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2361] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-9"
            - paragraph [ref=e2362]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2363] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-10"
            - paragraph [ref=e2364]:
              - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2365] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-11"
            - list [ref=e2366]:
              - listitem [ref=e2367]:
                - paragraph [ref=e2368]:
                  - text: "Send a reset segment:"
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2369] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10.5-12.1.1"
                - paragraph [ref=e2370]:
                  - text: <SEQ=SND.NXT><CTL=RST>
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2371] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10.5-12.1.2"
              - listitem [ref=e2372]:
                - text: All queued SENDs and RECEIVEs should be given "connection reset" notification; all segments queued for transmission (except for the RST formed above) or retransmission should be flushed. Delete the TCB, enter CLOSED state, and return.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2373] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.5-12.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2374]:
              - text: CLOSING STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2375] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-13"
            - paragraph [ref=e2376]:
              - text: LAST-ACK STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2377] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-14"
            - paragraph [ref=e2378]:
              - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2379] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.5-15"
            - list [ref=e2380]:
              - listitem [ref=e2381]:
                - text: Respond with "ok" and delete the TCB, enter CLOSED state, and return.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2382] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.5-16.1"
          - generic [ref=e2383]:
            - heading "3.10.6. STATUS Call" [level=4] [ref=e2384]:

              - link "STATUS Call" [ref=e2386] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-status-call"
            - paragraph [ref=e2387]:
              - text: CLOSED STATE (i.e., TCB does not exist)
              - link "¶" [ref=e2388] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.6-1"
            - list [ref=e2389]:
              - listitem [ref=e2390]:
                - text: "If the user should not have access to such a connection, return \"error: connection illegal for this process\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2391] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.6-2.1"
              - listitem [ref=e2392]:
                - text: "Otherwise, return \"error: connection does not exist\"."
                - link "¶" [ref=e2393] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.6-2.2"
            - paragraph [ref=e2394]:
              - text: LISTEN STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2395] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.6-3"
            - list [ref=e2396]:
              - listitem [ref=e2397]:
                - text: Return "state = LISTEN" and the TCB pointer.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2398] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.6-4.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2399]:
              - text: SYN-SENT STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2400] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.6-5"
            - list [ref=e2401]:
              - listitem [ref=e2402]:
                - text: Return "state = SYN-SENT" and the TCB pointer.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2403] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.6-6.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2404]:
              - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2405] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.6-7"
            - list [ref=e2406]:
              - listitem [ref=e2407]:
                - text: Return "state = SYN-RECEIVED" and the TCB pointer.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2408] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.6-8.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2409]:
              - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2410] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.6-9"
            - list [ref=e2411]:

                - text: Return "state = ESTABLISHED" and the TCB pointer.
                - link "¶" [ref=e2413] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.6-10.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e2414]:
              - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
              - link "¶" [ref=e2415] [cursor=pointer]:

            - paragraph [ref=e2419]:

                - link "¶" [ref=e2423] [cursor=pointer]:

            - paragraph [ref=e2424]:

                - link "¶" [ref=e2443] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.6-22.1"
          - generic [ref=e2444]:
            - heading "3.10.7. SEGMENT ARRIVES" [level=4] [ref=e2445]:
              - link "3.10.7." [ref=e2446] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.7"
              - link "SEGMENT ARRIVES" [ref=e2447] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-segment-arrives"
            - generic [ref=e2448]:
              - heading "3.10.7.1. CLOSED STATE" [level=5] [ref=e2449]:
                - link "3.10.7.1." [ref=e2450] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1"
                - link "CLOSED STATE" [ref=e2451] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-closed-state"
              - paragraph [ref=e2452]:
                - text: If the state is CLOSED (i.e., TCB does not exist), then
                - link "¶" [ref=e2453] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1-1"
              - list [ref=e2454]:
                - listitem [ref=e2455]:
                  - text: all data in the incoming segment is discarded. An incoming segment containing a RST is discarded. An incoming segment not containing a RST causes a RST to be sent in response. The acknowledgment and sequence field values are selected to make the reset sequence acceptable to the TCP endpoint that sent the offending segment.
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2456] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1-2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2457]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2458]:
                    - text: If the ACK bit is off, sequence number zero is used,
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2459] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1-2.2.1"
                  - list [ref=e2460]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2461]:
                      - text: <SEQ=0><ACK=SEG.SEQ+SEG.LEN><CTL=RST,ACK>
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2462] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1-2.2.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2463]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2464]:
                    - text: If the ACK bit is on,
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2465] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1-2.3.1"
                  - list [ref=e2466]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2467]:
                      - text: <SEQ=SEG.ACK><CTL=RST>
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2468] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1-2.3.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2469]:
                  - text: Return.
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2470] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.1-2.4"
            - generic [ref=e2471]:
              - heading "{{REDACTED_IP}}. LISTEN STATE" [level=5] [ref=e2472]:
                - link "3.10.7.2." [ref=e2473] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-{{REDACTED_IP}}"
                - link "LISTEN STATE" [ref=e2474] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-listen-state"
              - paragraph [ref=e2475]:
                - text: If the state is LISTEN, then
                - link "¶" [ref=e2476] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-1"
              - list [ref=e2477]:
                - listitem [ref=e2478]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2479]:
                    - text: "First, check for a RST:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2480] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.1.1"
                  - list [ref=e2481]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2482]:
                      - text: An incoming RST segment could not be valid since it could not have been sent in response to anything sent by this incarnation of the connection. An incoming RST should be ignored. Return.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2483] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.1.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2484]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2485]:
                    - text: "Second, check for an ACK:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2486] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.2.1"
                  - list [ref=e2487]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2488]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2489]:
                        - text: "Any acknowledgment is bad if it arrives on a connection still in the LISTEN state. An acceptable reset segment should be formed for any arriving ACK-bearing segment. The RST should be formatted as follows:"
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2490] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.2.2.1.1"
                      - list [ref=e2491]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2492]:
                          - text: <SEQ=SEG.ACK><CTL=RST>
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2493] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.2.2.1.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2494]:
                      - text: Return.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2495] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.2.2.2"
                - listitem [ref=e2496]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2497]:
                    - text: "Third, check for a SYN:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2498] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.3.1"
                  - list [ref=e2499]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2500]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2501]:
                        - text: If the SYN bit is set, check the security. If the security/compartment on the incoming segment does not exactly match the security/compartment in the TCB, then send a reset and return.
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2502] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.3.2.1.1"
                      - list [ref=e2503]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2504]:
                          - text: <SEQ=0><ACK=SEG.SEQ+SEG.LEN><CTL=RST,ACK>
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2505] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.3.2.1.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2506]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2507]:
                        - text: "Set RCV.NXT to SEG.SEQ+1, IRS is set to SEG.SEQ, and any other control or text should be queued for processing later. ISS should be selected and a SYN segment sent of the form:"
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2508] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.3.2.2.1"
                      - list [ref=e2509]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2510]:
                          - text: <SEQ=ISS><ACK=RCV.NXT><CTL=SYN,ACK>
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2511] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.3.2.2.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2512]:
                      - text: SND.NXT is set to ISS+1 and SND.UNA to ISS. The connection state should be changed to SYN-RECEIVED. Note that any other incoming control or data (combined with SYN) will be processed in the SYN-RECEIVED state, but processing of SYN and ACK should not be repeated. If the listen was not fully specified (i.e., the remote socket was not fully specified), then the unspecified fields should be filled in now.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2513] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.3.2.3"
                - listitem [ref=e2514]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2515]:
                    - text: "Fourth, other data or control:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2516] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.4.1"
                  - list [ref=e2517]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2518]:
                      - text: This should not be reached. Drop the segment and return. Any other control or data-bearing segment (not containing SYN) must have an ACK and thus would have been discarded by the ACK processing in the second step, unless it was first discarded by RST checking in the first step.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2519] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.2-2.4.2.1"
            - generic [ref=e2520]:
              - heading "3.10.7.3. SYN-SENT STATE" [level=5] [ref=e2521]:
                - link "3.10.7.3." [ref=e2522] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3"
                - link "SYN-SENT STATE" [ref=e2523] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-syn-sent-state"
              - paragraph [ref=e2524]:
                - text: If the state is SYN-SENT, then
                - link "¶" [ref=e2525] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-1"
              - list [ref=e2526]:
                - listitem [ref=e2527]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2528]:
                    - text: "First, check the ACK bit:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2529] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.1.1"
                  - list [ref=e2530]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2531]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2532]:
                        - text: If the ACK bit is set,
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2533] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.1.2.1.1"
                      - list [ref=e2534]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2535]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2536]:
                            - text: If SEG.ACK =< ISS or SEG.ACK > SND.NXT, send a reset (unless the RST bit is set, if so drop the segment and return)
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2537] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.1.2.1.2.1.1"
                          - list [ref=e2538]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2539]:
                              - text: <SEQ=SEG.ACK><CTL=RST>
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2540] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.1.2.1.2.1.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2541]:
                          - text: and discard the segment. Return.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2542] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.1.2.1.2.2"
                        - listitem [ref=e2543]:
                          - text: If SND.UNA < SEG.ACK =< SND.NXT, then the ACK is acceptable. Some deployed TCP code has used the check SEG.ACK == SND.NXT (using "==" rather than "=<"), but this is not appropriate when the stack is capable of sending data on the SYN because the TCP peer may not accept and acknowledge all of the data on the SYN.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2544] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.1.2.1.2.3"
                - listitem [ref=e2545]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2546]:
                    - text: "Second, check the RST bit:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2547] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.2.1"
                  - list [ref=e2548]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2549]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2550]:
                        - text: If the RST bit is set,
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2551] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.2.2.1.1"
                      - list [ref=e2552]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2553]:
                          - text: A potential blind reset attack is described in RFC 5961
                          - generic [ref=e2554]:
                            - text: "["
                            - link "9" [ref=e2555] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5961
                            - text: "]"
                          - text: . The mitigation described in that document has specific applicability explained therein, and is not a substitute for cryptographic protection (e.g., IPsec or TCP-AO). A TCP implementation that supports the mitigation described in RFC 5961 SHOULD first check that the sequence number exactly matches RCV.NXT prior to executing the action in the next paragraph.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2556] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.2.2.1.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2557]:
                          - text: "If the ACK was acceptable, then signal to the user \"error: connection reset\", drop the segment, enter CLOSED state, delete TCB, and return. Otherwise (no ACK), drop the segment and return."
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2558] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.2.2.1.2.2"
                - listitem [ref=e2559]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2560]:
                    - text: "Third, check the security:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2561] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.3.1"
                  - list [ref=e2562]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2563]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2564]:
                        - text: "If the security/compartment in the segment does not exactly match the security/compartment in the TCB, send a reset:"
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2565] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.3.2.1.1"
                      - list [ref=e2566]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2567]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2568]:
                            - text: If there is an ACK,
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2569] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.3.2.1.2.1.1"
                          - list [ref=e2570]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2571]:
                              - text: <SEQ=SEG.ACK><CTL=RST>
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2572] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.3.2.1.2.1.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2573]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2574]:
                            - text: Otherwise,
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2575] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.3.2.1.2.2.1"
                          - list [ref=e2576]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2577]:
                              - text: <SEQ=0><ACK=SEG.SEQ+SEG.LEN><CTL=RST,ACK>
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2578] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.3.2.1.2.2.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2579]:
                      - text: If a reset was sent, discard the segment and return.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2580] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.3.2.2"
                - listitem [ref=e2581]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2582]:
                    - text: "Fourth, check the SYN bit:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2583] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.1"
                  - list [ref=e2584]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2585]:
                      - text: This step should be reached only if the ACK is ok, or there is no ACK, and the segment did not contain a RST.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2586] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2587]:
                      - text: If the SYN bit is on and the security/compartment is acceptable, then RCV.NXT is set to SEG.SEQ+1, IRS is set to SEG.SEQ. SND.UNA should be advanced to equal SEG.ACK (if there is an ACK), and any segments on the retransmission queue that are thereby acknowledged should be removed.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2588] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.2"
                    - listitem [ref=e2589]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2590]:
                        - text: If SND.UNA > ISS (our SYN has been ACKed), change the connection state to ESTABLISHED, form an ACK segment
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2591] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.3.1"
                      - list [ref=e2592]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2593]:
                          - text: <SEQ=SND.NXT><ACK=RCV.NXT><CTL=ACK>
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2594] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.3.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2595]:
                      - text: and send it. Data or controls that were queued for transmission MAY be included. Some TCP implementations suppress sending this segment when the received segment contains data that will anyways generate an acknowledgment in the later processing steps, saving this extra acknowledgment of the SYN from being sent. If there are other controls or text in the segment, then continue processing at the
                      - link "sixth step" [ref=e2596] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#check-urg-bit"
                      - text: under
                      - link "Section 3.10.7.4" [ref=e2597] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#other-states"
                      - text: where the URG bit is checked; otherwise, return.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2598] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.4"
                    - listitem [ref=e2599]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2600]:
                        - text: Otherwise, enter SYN-RECEIVED, form a SYN,ACK segment
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2601] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.5.1"
                      - list [ref=e2602]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2603]:
                          - text: <SEQ=ISS><ACK=RCV.NXT><CTL=SYN,ACK>
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2604] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.5.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2605]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2606]:
                        - text: "and send it. Set the variables:"
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2607] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.6.1"
                      - list [ref=e2608]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2609]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2610]:
                            - text: SND.WND <- SEG.WND
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2611] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.6.2.1.1"
                          - paragraph [ref=e2612]:
                            - text: SND.WL1 <- SEG.SEQ
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2613] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.6.2.1.2"
                          - paragraph [ref=e2614]:
                            - text: SND.WL2 <- SEG.ACK
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2615] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.6.2.1.3"
                      - paragraph [ref=e2616]:
                        - text: If there are other controls or text in the segment, queue them for processing after the ESTABLISHED state has been reached, return.
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2617] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.6.3"
                    - listitem [ref=e2618]:
                      - text: Note that it is legal to send and receive application data on SYN segments (this is the "text in the segment" mentioned above). There has been significant misinformation and misunderstanding of this topic historically. Some firewalls and security devices consider this suspicious. However, the capability was used in T/TCP
                      - generic [ref=e2619]:
                        - text: "["
                        - link "21" [ref=e2620] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1644
                        - text: "]"
                      - text: and is used in TCP Fast Open (TFO)
                      - generic [ref=e2621]:
                        - text: "["
                        - link "48" [ref=e2622] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7413
                        - text: "]"
                      - text: ", so is important for implementations and network devices to permit."
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2623] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.4.2.7"
                - listitem [ref=e2624]:
                  - text: Fifth, if neither of the SYN or RST bits is set, then drop the segment and return.
                  - link "¶" [ref=e2625] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.3-2.5"
            - generic [ref=e2627]:
              - heading "3.10.7.4. Other States" [level=5] [ref=e2628]:
                - link "3.10.7.4." [ref=e2629] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4"
                - link "Other States" [ref=e2630] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#name-other-states"
              - paragraph [ref=e2631]:
                - text: Otherwise,
                - link "¶" [ref=e2632] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-1"
              - list [ref=e2633]:
                - listitem [ref=e2634]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2635]:
                    - text: "First, check sequence number:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2636] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.1"
                  - list [ref=e2637]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2638]:
                      - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2639] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2640]:
                      - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2641] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.2"
                    - listitem [ref=e2642]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2643] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.3"
                    - listitem [ref=e2644]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2645] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.4"
                    - listitem [ref=e2646]:
                      - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2647] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.5"
                    - listitem [ref=e2648]:
                      - text: CLOSING STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2649] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.6"
                    - listitem [ref=e2650]:
                      - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2651] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.7"
                    - listitem [ref=e2652]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2653]:
                        - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2654] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.1"
                      - list [ref=e2655]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2656]:
                          - text: Segments are processed in sequence. Initial tests on arrival are used to discard old duplicates, but further processing is done in SEG.SEQ order. If a segment's contents straddle the boundary between old and new, only the new parts are processed.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2657] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2658]:
                          - text: In general, the processing of received segments MUST be implemented to aggregate ACK segments whenever possible (MUST-58). For example, if the TCP endpoint is processing a series of queued segments, it MUST process them all before sending any ACK segments (MUST-59).
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2659] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.2"
                        - listitem [ref=e2660]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2661]:
                            - text: "There are four cases for the acceptability test for an incoming segment:"
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2662] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.3.1"
                          - 'table "Table 6: Segment Acceptability Tests" [ref=e2663]':
                            - caption [ref=e2664]:
                              - link "Table 6" [ref=e2665] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#table-6"
                              - text: ":"
                              - link "Segment Acceptability Tests" [ref=e2666] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#name-segment-acceptability-tests-2"
                            - rowgroup [ref=e2667]:
                              - row "Segment Length Receive Window Test" [ref=e2668]:
                                - columnheader "Segment Length" [ref=e2669]
                                - columnheader "Receive Window" [ref=e2670]
                                - columnheader "Test" [ref=e2671]
                            - rowgroup [ref=e2672]:
                              - row "0 0 SEG.SEQ = RCV.NXT" [ref=e2673]:
                                - cell "0" [ref=e2674]
                                - cell "0" [ref=e2675]
                                - cell "SEG.SEQ = RCV.NXT" [ref=e2676]
                              - row "0 >0 RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND" [ref=e2677]:
                                - cell "0" [ref=e2678]
                                - cell ">0" [ref=e2679]
                                - cell "RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND" [ref=e2680]
