                              - row ">0 0 not acceptable" [ref=e2681]:
                                - cell ">0" [ref=e2682]
                                - cell "0" [ref=e2683]
                                - cell "not acceptable" [ref=e2684]
                              - row ">0 >0 RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND ¶ or ¶ RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ+SEG.LEN-1 < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND ¶" [ref=e2685]:
                                - cell ">0" [ref=e2686]
                                - cell ">0" [ref=e2687]
                                - cell "RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND ¶ or ¶ RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ+SEG.LEN-1 < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND ¶" [ref=e2688]:
                                  - paragraph [ref=e2689]:
                                    - text: RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND
                                    - link "¶" [ref=e2690] [cursor=pointer]:
                                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.3.2.2.4.3.1"
                                  - paragraph [ref=e2691]:
                                    - text: or
                                    - link "¶" [ref=e2692] [cursor=pointer]:
                                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.3.2.2.4.3.2"
                                  - paragraph [ref=e2693]:
                                    - text: RCV.NXT =< SEG.SEQ+SEG.LEN-1 < RCV.NXT+RCV.WND
                                    - link "¶" [ref=e2694] [cursor=pointer]:
                                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.3.2.2.4.3.3"
                        - listitem [ref=e2695]:
                          - text: In implementing sequence number validation as described here, please note
                          - link "Appendix A.2" [ref=e2696] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#seqval"
                          - text: .
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2697] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.4"
                        - listitem [ref=e2698]:
                          - text: If the RCV.WND is zero, no segments will be acceptable, but special allowance should be made to accept valid ACKs, URGs, and RSTs.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2699] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.5"
                        - listitem [ref=e2700]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2701]:
                            - text: "If an incoming segment is not acceptable, an acknowledgment should be sent in reply (unless the RST bit is set, if so drop the segment and return):"
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2702] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.6.1"
                          - paragraph [ref=e2703]:
                            - text: <SEQ=SND.NXT><ACK=RCV.NXT><CTL=ACK>
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2704] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.6.2"
                        - listitem [ref=e2705]:
                          - text: After sending the acknowledgment, drop the unacceptable segment and return.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2706] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.7"
                        - listitem [ref=e2707]:
                          - text: Note that for the TIME-WAIT state, there is an improved algorithm described in
                          - generic [ref=e2708]:
                            - text: "["
                            - link "40" [ref=e2709] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6191
                            - text: "]"
                          - text: for handling incoming SYN segments that utilizes timestamps rather than relying on the sequence number check described here. When the improved algorithm is implemented, the logic above is not applicable for incoming SYN segments with Timestamp Options, received on a connection in the TIME-WAIT state.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2710] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.8"
                        - listitem [ref=e2711]:
                          - text: In the following it is assumed that the segment is the idealized segment that begins at RCV.NXT and does not exceed the window. One could tailor actual segments to fit this assumption by trimming off any portions that lie outside the window (including SYN and FIN) and only processing further if the segment then begins at RCV.NXT. Segments with higher beginning sequence numbers SHOULD be held for later processing (SHLD-31).
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2712] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.1.2.8.2.9"
                - listitem [ref=e2713]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2714]:
                    - text: "Second, check the RST bit:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2715] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.1"
                  - list [ref=e2716]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2717]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2718]:
                        - text: RFC 5961
                        - generic [ref=e2719]:
                          - text: "["
                          - link "9" [ref=e2720] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5961
                          - text: "]"
                        - text: ", Section"
                        - link "3" [ref=e2721] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5961#section-3
                        - text: describes a potential blind reset attack and optional mitigation approach. This does not provide a cryptographic protection (e.g., as in IPsec or TCP-AO) but can be applicable in situations described in RFC 5961. For stacks implementing the protection described in RFC 5961, the three checks below apply; otherwise, processing for these states is indicated further below.
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2722] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.1.1"
                      - generic [ref=e2723]:
                        - term [ref=e2724]: 1)
                        - definition [ref=e2725]:
                          - text: If the RST bit is set and the sequence number is outside the current receive window, silently drop the segment.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2726] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.1.2.1"
                        - term [ref=e2727]: 2)
                        - definition [ref=e2728]:
                          - text: If the RST bit is set and the sequence number exactly matches the next expected sequence number (RCV.NXT), then TCP endpoints MUST reset the connection in the manner prescribed below according to the connection state.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2729] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.1.2.2"
                        - term [ref=e2730]: 3)
                        - definition [ref=e2731]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2732]:
                            - text: "If the RST bit is set and the sequence number does not exactly match the next expected sequence value, yet is within the current receive window, TCP endpoints MUST send an acknowledgment (challenge ACK):"
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2733] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.1.2.3.1"
                          - paragraph [ref=e2734]:
                            - text: <SEQ=SND.NXT><ACK=RCV.NXT><CTL=ACK>
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2735] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.1.2.3.2"
                          - paragraph [ref=e2736]:
                            - text: After sending the challenge ACK, TCP endpoints MUST drop the unacceptable segment and stop processing the incoming packet further. Note that RFC 5961 and Errata ID 4772
                            - generic [ref=e2737]:
                              - text: "["
                              - link "99" [ref=e2738] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/errata/eid4772
                              - text: "]"
                            - text: contain additional considerations for ACK throttling in an implementation.
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2739] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.1.2.3.3"
                    - listitem [ref=e2740]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2741]:
                        - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2742] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.2.1"
                      - list [ref=e2743]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2744]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2745]:
                            - text: If the RST bit is set,
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2746] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.2.2.1.1"
                          - list [ref=e2747]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2748]:
                              - text: If this connection was initiated with a passive OPEN (i.e., came from the LISTEN state), then return this connection to LISTEN state and return. The user need not be informed. If this connection was initiated with an active OPEN (i.e., came from SYN-SENT state), then the connection was refused; signal the user "connection refused". In either case, the retransmission queue should be flushed. And in the active OPEN case, enter the CLOSED state and delete the TCB, and return.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2749] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2750]:
                      - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2751] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.3"
                    - listitem [ref=e2752]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2753] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.4"
                    - listitem [ref=e2754]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2755] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.5"
                    - listitem [ref=e2756]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2757]:
                        - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2758] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.6.1"
                      - list [ref=e2759]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2760]:
                          - text: If the RST bit is set, then any outstanding RECEIVEs and SEND should receive "reset" responses. All segment queues should be flushed. Users should also receive an unsolicited general "connection reset" signal. Enter the CLOSED state, delete the TCB, and return.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2761] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.6.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2762]:
                      - text: CLOSING STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2763] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.7"
                    - listitem [ref=e2764]:
                      - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2765] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.8"
                    - listitem [ref=e2766]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2767]:
                        - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2768] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.9.1"
                      - list [ref=e2769]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2770]:
                          - text: If the RST bit is set, then enter the CLOSED state, delete the TCB, and return.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2771] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.2.2.9.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2772]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2773]:
                    - text: "Third, check security:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2774] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.1"
                  - list [ref=e2775]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2776]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2777]:
                        - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2778] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.1.1"
                      - list [ref=e2779]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2780]:
                          - text: If the security/compartment in the segment does not exactly match the security/compartment in the TCB, then send a reset and return.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2781] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.1.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2782]:
                      - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2783] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.2"
                    - listitem [ref=e2784]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2785] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.3"
                    - listitem [ref=e2786]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2787] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.4"
                    - listitem [ref=e2788]:
                      - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2789] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.5"
                    - listitem [ref=e2790]:
                      - text: CLOSING STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2791] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.6"
                    - listitem [ref=e2792]:
                      - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2793] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.7"
                    - listitem [ref=e2794]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2795]:
                        - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2796] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.8.1"
                      - list [ref=e2797]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2798]:
                          - text: If the security/compartment in the segment does not exactly match the security/compartment in the TCB, then send a reset; any outstanding RECEIVEs and SEND should receive "reset" responses. All segment queues should be flushed. Users should also receive an unsolicited general "connection reset" signal. Enter the CLOSED state, delete the TCB, and return.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2799] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.8.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2800]:
                      - text: Note this check is placed following the sequence check to prevent a segment from an old connection between these port numbers with a different security from causing an abort of the current connection.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2801] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.3.2.9"
                - listitem [ref=e2802]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2803]:
                    - text: "Fourth, check the SYN bit:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2804] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.1"
                  - list [ref=e2805]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2806]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2807]:
                        - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2808] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.1.1"
                      - list [ref=e2809]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2810]:
                          - text: If the connection was initiated with a passive OPEN, then return this connection to the LISTEN state and return. Otherwise, handle per the directions for synchronized states below.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2811] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.1.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2812]:
                      - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2813] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.2"
                    - listitem [ref=e2814]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2815] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.3"
                    - listitem [ref=e2816]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2817] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.4"
                    - listitem [ref=e2818]:
                      - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2819] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.5"
                    - listitem [ref=e2820]:
                      - text: CLOSING STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2821] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.6"
                    - listitem [ref=e2822]:
                      - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2823] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.7"
                    - listitem [ref=e2824]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2825]:
                        - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2826] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.8.1"
                      - list [ref=e2827]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2828]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2829]:
                            - text: If the SYN bit is set in these synchronized states, it may be either a legitimate new connection attempt (e.g., in the case of TIME-WAIT), an error where the connection should be reset, or the result of an attack attempt, as described in RFC 5961
                            - generic [ref=e2830]:
                              - text: "["
                              - link "9" [ref=e2831] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5961
                              - text: "]"
                            - text: . For the TIME-WAIT state, new connections can be accepted if the Timestamp Option is used and meets expectations (per
                            - generic [ref=e2832]:
                              - text: "["
                              - link "40" [ref=e2833] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6191
                              - text: "]"
                            - text: ). For all other cases, RFC 5961 provides a mitigation with applicability to some situations, though there are also alternatives that offer cryptographic protection (see
                            - link "Section 7" [ref=e2834] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#Security"
                            - text: "). RFC 5961 recommends that in these synchronized states, if the SYN bit is set, irrespective of the sequence number, TCP endpoints MUST send a \"challenge ACK\" to the remote peer:"
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2835] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.8.2.1.1"
                          - paragraph [ref=e2836]:
                            - text: <SEQ=SND.NXT><ACK=RCV.NXT><CTL=ACK>
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2837] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.8.2.1.2"
                        - listitem [ref=e2838]:
                          - text: After sending the acknowledgment, TCP implementations MUST drop the unacceptable segment and stop processing further. Note that RFC 5961 and Errata ID 4772
                          - generic [ref=e2839]:
                            - text: "["
                            - link "99" [ref=e2840] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/errata/eid4772
                            - text: "]"
                          - text: contain additional ACK throttling notes for an implementation.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2841] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.8.2.2"
                        - listitem [ref=e2842]:
                          - text: "For implementations that do not follow RFC 5961, the original behavior described in RFC 793 follows in this paragraph. If the SYN is in the window it is an error: send a reset, any outstanding RECEIVEs and SEND should receive \"reset\" responses, all segment queues should be flushed, the user should also receive an unsolicited general \"connection reset\" signal, enter the CLOSED state, delete the TCB, and return."
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2843] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.8.2.3"
                        - listitem [ref=e2844]:
                          - text: If the SYN is not in the window, this step would not be reached and an ACK would have been sent in the first step (sequence number check).
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2845] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.4.2.8.2.4"
                - listitem [ref=e2846]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2847]:
                    - text: "Fifth, check the ACK field:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2848] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.1"
                  - list [ref=e2849]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2850]:
                      - text: if the ACK bit is off, drop the segment and return
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2851] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2852]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2853]:
                        - text: if the ACK bit is on,
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2854] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.1"
                      - list [ref=e2855]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2856]:
                          - text: RFC 5961
                          - generic [ref=e2857]:
                            - text: "["
                            - link "9" [ref=e2858] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5961
                            - text: "],"
                            - link "Section 5" [ref=e2859] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5961#section-5
                          - text: "describes a potential blind data injection attack, and mitigation that implementations MAY choose to include (MAY-12). TCP stacks that implement RFC 5961 MUST add an input check that the ACK value is acceptable only if it is in the range of ((SND.UNA - MAX.SND.WND) =< SEG.ACK =< SND.NXT). All incoming segments whose ACK value doesn't satisfy the above condition MUST be discarded and an ACK sent back. The new state variable MAX.SND.WND is defined as the largest window that the local sender has ever received from its peer (subject to window scaling) or may be hard-coded to a maximum permissible window value. When the ACK value is acceptable, the per-state processing below applies:"
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2860] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2861]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2862]:
                            - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2863] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.1"
                          - list [ref=e2864]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2865]:
                              - paragraph [ref=e2866]:
                                - text: "If SND.UNA < SEG.ACK =< SND.NXT, then enter ESTABLISHED state and continue processing with the variables below set to:"
                                - link "¶" [ref=e2867] [cursor=pointer]:
                                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.2.1.1"
                              - list [ref=e2868]:
                                - listitem [ref=e2869]:
                                  - text: SND.WND <- SEG.WND
                                  - link "¶" [ref=e2870] [cursor=pointer]:
                                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.1"
                                - listitem [ref=e2871]:
                                  - text: SND.WL1 <- SEG.SEQ
                                  - link "¶" [ref=e2872] [cursor=pointer]:
                                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.2"
                                - listitem [ref=e2873]:
                                  - text: SND.WL2 <- SEG.ACK
                                  - link "¶" [ref=e2874] [cursor=pointer]:
                                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.2.1.2.3"
                            - listitem [ref=e2875]:
                              - paragraph [ref=e2876]:
                                - text: If the segment acknowledgment is not acceptable, form a reset segment
                                - link "¶" [ref=e2877] [cursor=pointer]:
                                  - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.2.2.1"
                              - list [ref=e2878]:
                                - listitem [ref=e2879]:
                                  - text: <SEQ=SEG.ACK><CTL=RST>
                                  - link "¶" [ref=e2880] [cursor=pointer]:
                                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.2.2.2.1"
                            - listitem [ref=e2881]:
                              - text: and send it.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2882] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.2.2.3"
                        - listitem [ref=e2883]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2884]:
                            - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2885] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.3.1"
                          - list [ref=e2886]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2887]:
                              - text: If SND.UNA < SEG.ACK =< SND.NXT, then set SND.UNA <- SEG.ACK. Any segments on the retransmission queue that are thereby entirely acknowledged are removed. Users should receive positive acknowledgments for buffers that have been SENT and fully acknowledged (i.e., SEND buffer should be returned with "ok" response). If the ACK is a duplicate (SEG.ACK =< SND.UNA), it can be ignored. If the ACK acks something not yet sent (SEG.ACK > SND.NXT), then send an ACK, drop the segment, and return.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2888] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.3.2.1"
                            - listitem [ref=e2889]:
                              - text: If SND.UNA =< SEG.ACK =< SND.NXT, the send window should be updated. If (SND.WL1 < SEG.SEQ or (SND.WL1 = SEG.SEQ and SND.WL2 =< SEG.ACK)), set SND.WND <- SEG.WND, set SND.WL1 <- SEG.SEQ, and set SND.WL2 <- SEG.ACK.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2890] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.3.2.2"
                            - listitem [ref=e2891]:
                              - text: Note that SND.WND is an offset from SND.UNA, that SND.WL1 records the sequence number of the last segment used to update SND.WND, and that SND.WL2 records the acknowledgment number of the last segment used to update SND.WND. The check here prevents using old segments to update the window.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2892] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.3.2.3"
                        - listitem [ref=e2893]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2894]:
                            - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2895] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.4.1"
                          - list [ref=e2896]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2897]:
                              - text: In addition to the processing for the ESTABLISHED state, if the FIN segment is now acknowledged, then enter FIN-WAIT-2 and continue processing in that state.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2898] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.4.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2899]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2900]:
                            - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2901] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.5.1"
                          - list [ref=e2902]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2903]:
                              - text: In addition to the processing for the ESTABLISHED state, if the retransmission queue is empty, the user's CLOSE can be acknowledged ("ok") but do not delete the TCB.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2904] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.5.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2905]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2906]:
                            - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2907] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.6.1"
                          - list [ref=e2908]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2909]:
                              - text: Do the same processing as for the ESTABLISHED state.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2910] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.6.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2911]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2912]:
                            - text: CLOSING STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2913] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.7.1"
                          - list [ref=e2914]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2915]:
                              - text: In addition to the processing for the ESTABLISHED state, if the ACK acknowledges our FIN, then enter the TIME-WAIT state; otherwise, ignore the segment.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2916] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.7.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2917]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2918]:
                            - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2919] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.8.1"
                          - list [ref=e2920]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2921]:
                              - text: The only thing that can arrive in this state is an acknowledgment of our FIN. If our FIN is now acknowledged, delete the TCB, enter the CLOSED state, and return.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2922] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.8.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2923]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2924]:
                            - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2925] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.9.1"
                          - list [ref=e2926]:
                            - listitem [ref=e2927]:
                              - text: The only thing that can arrive in this state is a retransmission of the remote FIN. Acknowledge it, and restart the 2 MSL timeout.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e2928] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.5.2.2.2.9.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2929]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2931]:
                    - text: "Sixth, check the URG bit:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2932] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.1"
                  - list [ref=e2933]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2934]:
                      - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2935] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2936]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2937] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.2"
                    - listitem [ref=e2938]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2939]:
                        - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2940] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.3.1"
                      - list [ref=e2941]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2942]:
                          - text: If the URG bit is set, RCV.UP <- max(RCV.UP,SEG.UP), and signal the user that the remote side has urgent data if the urgent pointer (RCV.UP) is in advance of the data consumed. If the user has already been signaled (or is still in the "urgent mode") for this continuous sequence of urgent data, do not signal the user again.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2943] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.3.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2944]:
                      - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2945] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.4"
                    - listitem [ref=e2946]:
                      - text: CLOSING STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2947] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.5"
                    - listitem [ref=e2948]:
                      - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2949] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.6"
                    - listitem [ref=e2950]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2951]:
                        - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2952] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.7.1"
                      - list [ref=e2953]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2954]:
                          - text: This should not occur since a FIN has been received from the remote side. Ignore the URG.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2955] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.6.2.7.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2956]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2957]:
                    - text: "Seventh, process the segment text:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e2958] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.1"
                  - list [ref=e2959]:
                    - listitem [ref=e2960]:
                      - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2961] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e2962]:
                      - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2963] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.2"
                    - listitem [ref=e2964]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2965]:
                        - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2966] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.1"
                      - list [ref=e2967]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2968]:
                          - text: Once in the ESTABLISHED state, it is possible to deliver segment data to user RECEIVE buffers. Data from segments can be moved into buffers until either the buffer is full or the segment is empty. If the segment empties and carries a PUSH flag, then the user is informed, when the buffer is returned, that a PUSH has been received.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2969] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e2970]:
                          - text: When the TCP endpoint takes responsibility for delivering the data to the user, it must also acknowledge the receipt of the data.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2971] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.2"
                        - listitem [ref=e2972]:
                          - text: Once the TCP endpoint takes responsibility for the data, it advances RCV.NXT over the data accepted, and adjusts RCV.WND as appropriate to the current buffer availability. The total of RCV.NXT and RCV.WND should not be reduced.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2973] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.3"
                        - listitem [ref=e2974]:
                          - text: A TCP implementation MAY send an ACK segment acknowledging RCV.NXT when a valid segment arrives that is in the window but not at the left window edge (MAY-13).
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2975] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.4"
                        - listitem [ref=e2976]:
                          - text: Please note the window management suggestions in
                          - link "Section 3.8" [ref=e2977] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#datacomm"
                          - text: .
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2978] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.5"
                        - listitem [ref=e2979]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e2980]:
                            - text: "Send an acknowledgment of the form:"
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2981] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.6.1"
                          - paragraph [ref=e2982]:
                            - text: <SEQ=SND.NXT><ACK=RCV.NXT><CTL=ACK>
                            - link "¶" [ref=e2983] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.6.2"
                        - listitem [ref=e2984]:
                          - text: This acknowledgment should be piggybacked on a segment being transmitted if possible without incurring undue delay.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2985] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.3.2.7"
                    - listitem [ref=e2986]:
                      - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2987] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.4"
                    - listitem [ref=e2988]:
                      - text: CLOSING STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2989] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.5"
                    - listitem [ref=e2990]:
                      - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                      - link "¶" [ref=e2991] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.6"
                    - listitem [ref=e2992]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e2993]:
                        - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                        - link "¶" [ref=e2994] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.7.1"
                      - list [ref=e2995]:
                        - listitem [ref=e2996]:
                          - text: This should not occur since a FIN has been received from the remote side. Ignore the segment text.
                          - link "¶" [ref=e2997] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.7.2.7.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e2998]:
                  - paragraph [ref=e2999]:
                    - text: "Eighth, check the FIN bit:"
                    - link "¶" [ref=e3000] [cursor=pointer]:
                      - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.1"
                  - list [ref=e3001]:
                    - listitem [ref=e3002]:
                      - text: Do not process the FIN if the state is CLOSED, LISTEN, or SYN-SENT since the SEG.SEQ cannot be validated; drop the segment and return.
                      - link "¶" [ref=e3003] [cursor=pointer]:
                        - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.1"
                    - listitem [ref=e3004]:
                      - paragraph [ref=e3005]:
                        - text: If the FIN bit is set, signal the user "connection closing" and return any pending RECEIVEs with same message, advance RCV.NXT over the FIN, and send an acknowledgment for the FIN. Note that FIN implies PUSH for any segment text not yet delivered to the user.
                        - link "¶" [ref=e3006] [cursor=pointer]:
                          - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.1"
                      - list [ref=e3007]:
                        - listitem [ref=e3008]:
                          - text: SYN-RECEIVED STATE
                          - link "¶" [ref=e3009] [cursor=pointer]:
                            - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e3010]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e3011]:
                            - text: ESTABLISHED STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e3012] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.2.1"
                          - list [ref=e3013]:
                            - listitem [ref=e3014]:
                              - text: Enter the CLOSE-WAIT state.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e3015] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.2.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e3016]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e3017]:
                            - text: FIN-WAIT-1 STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e3018] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.3.1"
                          - list [ref=e3019]:
                            - listitem [ref=e3020]:
                              - text: If our FIN has been ACKed (perhaps in this segment), then enter TIME-WAIT, start the time-wait timer, turn off the other timers; otherwise, enter the CLOSING state.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e3021] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.3.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e3022]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e3023]:
                            - text: FIN-WAIT-2 STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e3024] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.4.1"
                          - list [ref=e3025]:
                            - listitem [ref=e3026]:
                              - text: Enter the TIME-WAIT state. Start the time-wait timer, turn off the other timers.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e3027] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.4.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e3028]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e3029]:
                            - text: CLOSE-WAIT STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e3030] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.5.1"
                          - list [ref=e3031]:
                            - listitem [ref=e3032]:
                              - text: Remain in the CLOSE-WAIT state.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e3033] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.5.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e3034]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e3035]:
                            - text: CLOSING STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e3036] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.6.1"
                          - list [ref=e3037]:
                            - listitem [ref=e3038]:
                              - text: Remain in the CLOSING state.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e3039] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.6.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e3040]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e3041]:
                            - text: LAST-ACK STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e3042] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.7.1"
                          - list [ref=e3043]:
                            - listitem [ref=e3044]:
                              - text: Remain in the LAST-ACK state.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e3045] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.7.2.1"
                        - listitem [ref=e3046]:
                          - paragraph [ref=e3047]:
                            - text: TIME-WAIT STATE
                            - link "¶" [ref=e3048] [cursor=pointer]:
                              - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.8.1"
                          - list [ref=e3049]:
                            - listitem [ref=e3050]:
                              - text: Remain in the TIME-WAIT state. Restart the 2 MSL time-wait timeout.
                              - link "¶" [ref=e3051] [cursor=pointer]:
                                - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.8.2.2.2.8.2.1"
                - listitem [ref=e3052]:
                  - text: and return.
                  - link "¶" [ref=e3053] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: "#section-3.10.7.4-2.9"
          - generic [ref=e3054]:
            - heading "3.10.8. Timeouts" [level=4] [ref=e3055]:
              - link "3.10.8." [ref=e3056] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.8"
              - link "Timeouts" [ref=e3057] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#name-timeouts"
            - paragraph [ref=e3058]:
              - text: USER TIMEOUT
              - link "¶" [ref=e3059] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.8-1"
            - list [ref=e3060]:
              - listitem [ref=e3061]:
                - text: "For any state if the user timeout expires, flush all queues, signal the user \"error: connection aborted due to user timeout\" in general and for any outstanding calls, delete the TCB, enter the CLOSED state, and return."
                - link "¶" [ref=e3062] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.8-2.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e3063]:
              - text: RETRANSMISSION TIMEOUT
              - link "¶" [ref=e3064] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.8-3"
            - list [ref=e3065]:
              - listitem [ref=e3066]:
                - text: For any state if the retransmission timeout expires on a segment in the retransmission queue, send the segment at the front of the retransmission queue again, reinitialize the retransmission timer, and return.
                - link "¶" [ref=e3067] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.8-4.1"
            - paragraph [ref=e3068]:
              - text: TIME-WAIT TIMEOUT
              - link "¶" [ref=e3069] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#section-3.10.8-5"
            - list [ref=e3070]:
              - listitem [ref=e3071]:
                - text: If the time-wait timeout expires on a connection, delete the TCB, enter the CLOSED state, and return.
                - link "¶" [ref=e3072] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: "#section-3.10.8-6.1"
      - generic [ref=e3074]:
        - heading "4. Glossary" [level=2] [ref=e3075]:
          - link "4." [ref=e3076] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: "#section-4"
          - link "Glossary" [ref=e3077] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: "#name-glossary"
        - generic [ref=e3078]:
          - term [ref=e3079]: ACK
          - definition [ref=e3080]:
            - text: A control bit (acknowledge) occupying no sequence space, which indicates that the acknowledgment field of this segment specifies the next sequence number the sender of this segment is expecting to receive, hence acknowledging receipt of all previous sequence numbers.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3081] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.2"
          - term [ref=e3082]: connection
          - definition [ref=e3083]:
            - text: A logical communication path identified by a pair of sockets.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3084] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.4"
          - term [ref=e3085]: datagram
          - definition [ref=e3086]:
            - text: A message sent in a packet-switched computer communications network.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3087] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.6"
          - term [ref=e3088]: Destination Address
          - definition [ref=e3089]:
            - text: The network-layer address of the endpoint intended to receive a segment.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3090] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.8"
          - term [ref=e3091]: FIN
          - definition [ref=e3092]:
            - text: A control bit (finis) occupying one sequence number, which indicates that the sender will send no more data or control occupying sequence space.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3093] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.10"
          - term [ref=e3094]: flush
          - definition [ref=e3095]:
            - text: To remove all of the contents (data or segments) from a store (buffer or queue).
            - link "¶" [ref=e3096] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.12"
          - term [ref=e3097]: fragment
          - definition [ref=e3098]:
            - text: A portion of a logical unit of data. In particular, an internet fragment is a portion of an internet datagram.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3099] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.14"
          - term [ref=e3100]: header
          - definition [ref=e3101]:
            - text: Control information at the beginning of a message, segment, fragment, packet, or block of data.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3102] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.16"
          - term [ref=e3103]: host
          - definition [ref=e3104]:
            - text: A computer. In particular, a source or destination of messages from the point of view of the communication network.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3105] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.18"
          - term [ref=e3106]: Identification
          - definition [ref=e3107]:
            - text: An Internet Protocol field. This identifying value assigned by the sender aids in assembling the fragments of a datagram.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3108] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.20"
          - term [ref=e3109]: internet address
          - definition [ref=e3110]:
            - text: A network-layer address.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3111] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.22"
          - term [ref=e3112]: internet datagram
          - definition [ref=e3113]:
            - text: A unit of data exchanged between internet hosts, together with the internet header that allows the datagram to be routed from source to destination.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3114] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.24"
          - term [ref=e3115]: internet fragment
          - definition [ref=e3116]:
            - text: A portion of the data of an internet datagram with an internet header.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3117] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.26"
          - term [ref=e3118]: IP
          - definition [ref=e3119]:
            - text: Internet Protocol. See
            - generic [ref=e3120]:
              - text: "["
              - link "1" [ref=e3121] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc791
              - text: "]"
            - text: and
            - generic [ref=e3122]:
              - text: "["
              - link "13" [ref=e3123] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8200
              - text: "]"
            - text: .
            - link "¶" [ref=e3124] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.28"
          - term [ref=e3125]: IRS
          - definition [ref=e3126]:
            - text: The Initial Receive Sequence number. The first sequence number used by the sender on a connection.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3127] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.30"
          - term [ref=e3128]: ISN
          - definition [ref=e3129]:
            - text: The Initial Sequence Number. The first sequence number used on a connection (either ISS or IRS). Selected in a way that is unique within a given period of time and is unpredictable to attackers.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3130] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.32"
          - term [ref=e3131]: ISS
          - definition [ref=e3132]:
            - text: The Initial Send Sequence number. The first sequence number used by the sender on a connection.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3133] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.34"
          - term [ref=e3134]: left sequence
          - definition [ref=e3135]:
            - text: This is the next sequence number to be acknowledged by the data-receiving TCP endpoint (or the lowest currently unacknowledged sequence number) and is sometimes referred to as the left edge of the send window.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3136] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.36"
          - term [ref=e3137]: module
          - definition [ref=e3138]:
            - text: An implementation, usually in software, of a protocol or other procedure.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3139] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.38"
          - term [ref=e3140]: MSL
          - definition [ref=e3141]:
            - text: Maximum Segment Lifetime, the time a TCP segment can exist in the internetwork system. Arbitrarily defined to be 2 minutes.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3142] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.40"
          - term [ref=e3143]: octet
          - definition [ref=e3144]:
            - text: An eight-bit byte.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3145] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.42"
          - term [ref=e3146]: Options
          - definition [ref=e3147]:
            - text: An Option field may contain several options, and each option may be several octets in length.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3148] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.44"
          - term [ref=e3149]: packet
          - definition [ref=e3150]:
            - text: A package of data with a header that may or may not be logically complete. More often a physical packaging than a logical packaging of data.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3151] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.46"
          - term [ref=e3152]: port
          - definition [ref=e3153]:
            - text: The portion of a connection identifier used for demultiplexing connections at an endpoint.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3154] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.48"
          - term [ref=e3155]: process
          - definition [ref=e3156]:
            - text: A program in execution. A source or destination of data from the point of view of the TCP endpoint or other host-to-host protocol.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3157] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.50"
          - term [ref=e3158]: PUSH
          - definition [ref=e3159]:
            - text: A control bit occupying no sequence space, indicating that this segment contains data that must be pushed through to the receiving user.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3160] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.52"
          - term [ref=e3161]: RCV.NXT
          - definition [ref=e3162]:
            - text: receive next sequence number
            - link "¶" [ref=e3163] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.54"
          - term [ref=e3164]: RCV.UP
          - definition [ref=e3165]:
            - text: receive urgent pointer
            - link "¶" [ref=e3166] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.56"
          - term [ref=e3167]: RCV.WND
          - definition [ref=e3168]:
            - text: receive window
            - link "¶" [ref=e3169] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.58"
          - term [ref=e3170]: receive next sequence number
          - definition [ref=e3171]:
            - text: This is the next sequence number the local TCP endpoint is expecting to receive.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3172] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.60"
          - term [ref=e3173]: receive window
          - definition [ref=e3174]:
            - text: This represents the sequence numbers the local (receiving) TCP endpoint is willing to receive. Thus, the local TCP endpoint considers that segments overlapping the range RCV.NXT to RCV.NXT + RCV.WND - 1 carry acceptable data or control. Segments containing sequence numbers entirely outside this range are considered duplicates or injection attacks and discarded.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3175] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.62"
          - term [ref=e3176]: RST
          - definition [ref=e3177]:
            - text: A control bit (reset), occupying no sequence space, indicating that the receiver should delete the connection without further interaction. The receiver can determine, based on the sequence number and acknowledgment fields of the incoming segment, whether it should honor the reset command or ignore it. In no case does receipt of a segment containing RST give rise to a RST in response.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3178] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.64"
          - term [ref=e3179]: SEG.ACK
          - definition [ref=e3180]:
            - text: segment acknowledgment
            - link "¶" [ref=e3181] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.66"
          - term [ref=e3182]: SEG.LEN
          - definition [ref=e3183]:
            - text: segment length
            - link "¶" [ref=e3184] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.68"
          - term [ref=e3185]: SEG.SEQ
          - definition [ref=e3186]:
            - text: segment sequence
            - link "¶" [ref=e3187] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.70"
          - term [ref=e3188]: SEG.UP
          - definition [ref=e3189]:
            - text: segment urgent pointer field
            - link "¶" [ref=e3190] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.72"
          - term [ref=e3191]: SEG.WND
          - definition [ref=e3192]:
            - text: segment window field
            - link "¶" [ref=e3193] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.74"
          - term [ref=e3194]: segment
          - definition [ref=e3195]:
            - text: A logical unit of data. In particular, a TCP segment is the unit of data transferred between a pair of TCP modules.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3196] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.76"
          - term [ref=e3197]: segment acknowledgment
          - definition [ref=e3198]:
            - text: The sequence number in the acknowledgment field of the arriving segment.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3199] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.78"
          - term [ref=e3200]: segment length
          - definition [ref=e3201]:
            - text: The amount of sequence number space occupied by a segment, including any controls that occupy sequence space.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3202] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.80"
          - term [ref=e3203]: segment sequence
          - definition [ref=e3204]:
            - text: The number in the sequence field of the arriving segment.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3205] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.82"
          - term [ref=e3206]: send sequence
          - definition [ref=e3207]:
            - text: This is the next sequence number the local (sending) TCP endpoint will use on the connection. It is initially selected from an initial sequence number curve (ISN) and is incremented for each octet of data or sequenced control transmitted.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3208] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.84"
          - term [ref=e3209]: send window
          - definition [ref=e3210]:
            - text: This represents the sequence numbers that the remote (receiving) TCP endpoint is willing to receive. It is the value of the window field specified in segments from the remote (data-receiving) TCP endpoint. The range of new sequence numbers that may be emitted by a TCP implementation lies between SND.NXT and SND.UNA + SND.WND - 1. (Retransmissions of sequence numbers between SND.UNA and SND.NXT are expected, of course.)
            - link "¶" [ref=e3211] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.86"
          - term [ref=e3212]: SND.NXT
          - definition [ref=e3213]:
            - text: send sequence
            - link "¶" [ref=e3214] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.88"
          - term [ref=e3215]: SND.UNA
          - definition [ref=e3216]:
            - text: left sequence
            - link "¶" [ref=e3217] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.90"
          - term [ref=e3218]: SND.UP
          - definition [ref=e3219]:
            - text: send urgent pointer
            - link "¶" [ref=e3220] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.92"
          - term [ref=e3221]: SND.WL1
          - definition [ref=e3222]:
            - text: segment sequence number at last window update
            - link "¶" [ref=e3223] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.94"
          - term [ref=e3224]: SND.WL2
          - definition [ref=e3225]:
            - text: segment acknowledgment number at last window update
            - link "¶" [ref=e3226] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.96"
          - term [ref=e3227]: SND.WND
          - definition [ref=e3228]:
            - text: send window
            - link "¶" [ref=e3229] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.98"
          - term [ref=e3230]: socket (or socket number, or socket address, or socket identifier)
          - definition [ref=e3231]:
            - text: An address that specifically includes a port identifier, that is, the concatenation of an Internet Address with a TCP port.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3232] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.100"
          - term [ref=e3233]: Source Address
          - definition [ref=e3234]:
            - text: The network-layer address of the sending endpoint.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3235] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.102"
          - term [ref=e3236]: SYN
          - definition [ref=e3237]:
            - text: A control bit in the incoming segment, occupying one sequence number, used at the initiation of a connection to indicate where the sequence numbering will start.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3238] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.104"
          - term [ref=e3239]: TCB
          - definition [ref=e3240]:
            - text: Transmission control block, the data structure that records the state of a connection.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3241] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.106"
          - term [ref=e3242]: TCP
          - definition [ref=e3243]:
            - text: "Transmission Control Protocol: a host-to-host protocol for reliable communication in internetwork environments."
            - link "¶" [ref=e3244] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.108"
          - term [ref=e3245]: TOS
          - definition [ref=e3246]:
            - text: Type of Service, an obsoleted IPv4 field. The same header bits currently are used for the Differentiated Services field
            - generic [ref=e3247]:
              - text: "["
              - link "4" [ref=e3248] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2474
              - text: "]"
            - text: containing the Differentiated Services Codepoint (DSCP) value and the 2-bit ECN codepoint
            - generic [ref=e3249]:
              - text: "["
              - link "6" [ref=e3250] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc3168
              - text: "]"
            - text: .
            - link "¶" [ref=e3251] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.110"
          - term [ref=e3252]: Type of Service
          - definition [ref=e3253]:
            - text: See "TOS".
            - link "¶" [ref=e3254] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.112"
          - term [ref=e3255]: URG
          - definition [ref=e3256]:
            - text: A control bit (urgent), occupying no sequence space, used to indicate that the receiving user should be notified to do urgent processing as long as there is data to be consumed with sequence numbers less than the value indicated by the urgent pointer.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3257] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.114"
          - term [ref=e3258]: urgent pointer
          - definition [ref=e3259]:
            - text: A control field meaningful only when the URG bit is on. This field communicates the value of the urgent pointer that indicates the data octet associated with the sending user's urgent call.
            - link "¶" [ref=e3260] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: "#section-4-1.116"
      - generic [ref=e3262]:
        - heading "5. Changes from RFC 793" [level=2] [ref=e3263]:
          - link "5." [ref=e3264] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: "#section-5"
          - link "Changes from RFC 793" [ref=e3265] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: "#name-changes-from-rfc-793"
        - paragraph [ref=e3266]:
          - text: This document obsoletes RFC 793 as well as RFCs 6093 and 6528, which updated 793. In all cases, only the normative protocol specification and requirements have been incorporated into this document, and some informational text with background and rationale may not have been carried in. The informational content of those documents is still valuable in learning about and understanding TCP, and they are valid Informational references, even though their normative content has been incorporated into this document.
          - link "¶" [ref=e3267] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: "#section-5-1"
        - paragraph [ref=e3268]:
          - text: The main body of this document was adapted from RFC 793's Section
          - link "3" [ref=e3269] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc793#section-3
          - text: ", titled \"FUNCTIONAL SPECIFICATION\", with an attempt to keep formatting and layout as close as possible."
          - link "¶" [ref=e3270] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: "#section-5-2"
        - paragraph [ref=e3271]:
          - text: "The collection of applicable RFC errata that have been reported and either accepted or held for an update to RFC 793 were incorporated (Errata IDs: 573"

            - text: "]"
          - text: ", 3305"
          - generic [ref=e3320]:
